$comment: Save-compatibility manifest (issue #766, save-overhaul C4). Tracks every save-format baseline this build is declared to keep loadable through explicit migrations, plus the tracked fixtures proving it. Never hand-edit a fixture's sha256 after it has been recorded here -- see tools/save_compat_audit.py (--add-baseline registers a fixture's checksum/summary together).
frozenDtoFingerprintComment: sha256 over every frozen `data ... deriving (...)` block's field list (whitespace-normalized) in SessionV90.hs -- see tools/save_compat_audit.py's frozen_dto_fingerprint(). Changes only when a field is added/removed/reordered, never on a comment-only edit. Recompute with: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'tools'); from save_compat_audit import frozen_dto_fingerprint; print(frozen_dto_fingerprint())"

baselines.description: The first tracked envelope (issue #759, save-overhaul B1): a single required "session" component wrapping the then-current SaveData/WorldPageSave shape (save-version 90) verbatim, alongside the ordinary "metadata" component. This is the compatibility baseline requirement 1 designates as the starting point -- every later baseline builds on it.

fixtures.provenance: Generated through the real codec: a genuine world.init('main_world', 42, 8, 3) small world was generated and saved through a real headless engine boot (so its WorldGenParams/plate data is staging-valid, unlike a hand-built defaultWorldGenParams value), its 'world-pages' component's real PageCoreDTO gen params were extracted, then wrapped as a v90-shaped SaveDataV90 value and encoded via World.Save.Envelope.Codec.encodeEnvelope into a single required 'session' component -- see tools/save_compat_audit.py --add-baseline to re-register after regenerating.

baselines.description: The raw-to-typed persistent-reference transition (issue #764, save-overhaul C3): craft-bills/power-nodes/unit-sim were bumped from v1 (bare BuildingId/UnitId references) to v2 (SamePageRef-typed references), each with an explicit v1->v2 migration (migrateCraftBillDTOv1/migratePowerNodesDTOv1/migrateUnitSimDTOv1). This baseline needs NO new migration code from this issue -- the existing ccInputVers=[1,2]-dispatched decode in World.Save.Component.Entities already fully supports it; this entry exists so removing that v1 decode support (or the migration functions) fails the compatibility audit, and so the fixture proves the dispatch through the REAL envelope/registry path, not just the isolated migrate* unit tests.

fixtures.provenance: Generated through the real codec: a single page (real, staging-valid WorldGenParams extracted from a genuine world.init('main_world', 42, 8, 3) headless-engine boot, same source as the b1-initial-session fixture) carrying one REAL-content building (def 'cargo_hold_S'), one REAL-content unit (def 'acolyte', matching what tools/multiworld_save_probe.py spawns), one unit-sim state, one craft bill (a real recipe id 'forge_steel_dagger', FixedCount mode so no output-item def is needed), and one power node -- built via World.Save.Snapshot.captureSessionSnapshot, encoded via World.Save.Component.encodeComponentSpecs, with craft-bills/power-nodes/unit-sim's payloads swapped for their historical v1 (bare-id) DTOs (CraftBillDTOv1/PowerNodeDTOv1/PageSimDTOv1, already exported by World.Save.Component.Entities), PLUS the real lua.unit_ai/lua.building_spawn components (empty AI/spawn state, version 3) extracted from that same real engine boot's save. Real content definitions (not the pure-hspec 'test_building'/'test_unit' placeholders) are required so the real engine's content-reference validation (Engine.Scripting.Lua.API.Save's continueLoad, issue #760 requirement 9) accepts this fixture in the fresh-process migration probe, not just the pure hspec decode path.

Generated through the real codec (issue #766 requirement 11: 'Include multi-page ... coverage', round-4 review): c3-typed-reference-v1-minimal's fixture was decoded via World.Save.Envelope.decodeSessionEnvelope to recover its REAL (SaveMetadata, SessionSnapshot), a SECOND page ('page2', deliberately empty -- no entities, so no building/unit id needs a fresh cross-page-unique allocation -- with its own distinct clock, 08:30 vs page1's 12:00) was inserted into snapPages/snapVisiblePages, then the whole session was re-encoded via World.Save.Envelope.encodeSessionSnapshot with page1's real content and Lua components (lua.unit_ai/lua.building_spawn, v3) carried over unchanged. Proves the manifest-driven hspec gate and the fresh-process migration probe genuinely assemble/reassemble/structurally-compare EVERY page in a session, not merely the first/only page every other tracked fixture happens to have.

baselines.description: The first versioned Lua persistence components (issue #761, save-overhaul B3): each registered persistent Lua module (unit_ai, building_spawn) became its own dynamically-added envelope component with an explicit version/inputVersions/decode/validate contract, mirroring the Haskell registry. Issue #764 (save-overhaul C3) then added a v1->v3 reference-typing migration on TOP of that same Lua contract (wrapUnitState/addOwnerToAiState etc. in scripts/unit_ai_save_refs.lua). This baseline's tracked fixtures below are REAL v1-shaped payloads (bare-number reference fields, no __owner) for both modules, encoded through the genuine scripts/lib/data_codec.lua M.encode via a real HsLua VM (not a hand-rolled reimplementation of the wire format, and not re-synthesized inline the way every OTHER Lua persistence test in this suite does) -- see Test.Headless.Lua.SaveModules's "tracked v1 fixtures from disk" describe block, which reads these exact bytes off disk and runs them through the real saveModules.prepareLoad/applyAll path, asserting the canonical unwrapped result these fixtures' .expected.json files record. The version recorded below (1) is deliberately the OLDEST accepted input version, not the current one (3) -- mirroring the c3-raw-reference-v1 baseline's convention -- since proving the oldest migration still works is this manifest's whole point; the current v1/v2/v3 contract itself remains exercised by every other 'Lua persistence components' test.

fixtures.provenance: Generated through the real codec: a v1-shaped unit_ai payload table ({[7] = {attackTargetUid=8, buildTarget=20, craftJob={billId=3, bid=21, recipeId='x'}}}, matching the same shape Test.Headless.Lua.SaveModules's inline v1-payload tests already use) encoded via scripts/lib/data_codec.lua's M.encode, run through a genuine HsLua VM (cabal repl, Lua.run/Lua.dostring -- the SAME entry points collectLuaComponents uses in Engine.Scripting.Lua.API.Save), not a Python or hand-written reimplementation of the wire format.

Generated through the real codec: a v1-shaped building_spawn payload table ({[12] = {lastUid=4, lastSpawnedAt=123.5}}, bare-number lastUid) encoded via scripts/lib/data_codec.lua's M.encode, run through a genuine HsLua VM the same way as lua-unit-ai-v1 above.

Generated through the real codec (round-3 review: a component-focused Lua fixture alone left the manifest-driven hspec gate and the fresh-process migration probe both skipping legacy Lua coverage entirely): docs/save_compat/manifest.json's c3-typed-reference-v1-minimal fixture was decoded via World.Save.Envelope.decodeSessionEnvelope to recover its REAL (SaveMetadata, SessionSnapshot) -- the same real cargo_hold_S building (id 1) and acolyte unit (id 1) -- then re-encoded via World.Save.Envelope.encodeSessionSnapshot with its lua.unit_ai/lua.building_spawn components replaced by v1-shaped payloads ({[1]={buildTarget=1}} / {[1]={lastUid=1}}, referencing that SAME real building/unit id) encoded through scripts/lib/data_codec.lua's M.encode via a genuine HsLua VM, mirroring lua-unit-ai-v1/lua-building-spawn-v1's generation above. This is a full complete-session envelope, not a lone component payload, so it is picked up automatically by BOTH Test.Headless.World.Save.Compat's manifest-driven "manifest-declared fixtures" gate (proving the Haskell envelope layer preserves a v1-tagged Lua component through decode/re-encode) and tools/save_compat_migration_probe.py's real-engine load->publish->resave->restart->reload round trip (proving saveModules.prepareLoad's real v1->v3 Lua migration fires inside a genuine engine boot, for a session the real content-reference validator also accepts).

Not a tracked binary fixture -- this points at the existing, already-blocking hspec gate ("Lua persistence components", part of cabal test synarchy-test-headless) that exercises unit_ai/building_spawn's FULL v1/v2/v3 decode+migrate+validate contract (including the two tracked fixtures above, plus every other historical shape and rejection case) directly through a standalone Lua VM (HsLua). Removing that coverage, or the v1/v2 decode branches it tests, fails the SAME cabal test synarchy-test-headless gate this manifest's own fixtures run under.

baselines.description: A REAL B1-era envelope recovered from git history (commit 988c2727, the tracked fixture Test.Headless.World.Save.Envelope carried before #760 replaced it) -- proves World.Save.Compat.SessionV90's frozen DTO decodes genuine pre-#760 bytes, not merely this codebase's own encoder output. This fixture's own "metadata" component was hand-picked independently of its gameplay gen params by the original test author (world size 64 / plate count 3, vs. the page's real defaultWorldGenParams 128 / 10) -- B1 predates requirement 12's manifest/gameplay agreement check entirely, so the FULL migration (decodeSessionEnvelope) correctly rejects it on that pre-existing mismatch. It is tracked as decode-only evidence, not as a fixture the full migration pipeline must accept.
baselines.supportStatus: decode-only (component-focused; not a migration-acceptance fixture -- see description)

fixtures.provenance: Recovered verbatim from git history: commit 988c2727 ("Introduce the tagged, checksummed save envelope (#759, save-overhaul B1)"), Test.Headless.World.Save.Envelope's trackedEnvelopeFixtureHex constant. Stored as an inline hex literal (matching that historical file's own convention) rather than a tracked binary blob, since a `git show` of that exact commit is the authoritative, independently-reproducible source -- see the module haddock.

retiredBaselines.description: The brief #760-only shape (Haskell persistence already split into independently-versioned components, but Lua state still the single opaque "lua-state" blob map predating #761's versioned Lua components). This window was NEVER declared a compatibility baseline -- no manifest existed yet, and no fixture for it was ever tracked outside the same-day #760/#761 development arc it was superseded within (#760's own fixture was itself replaced before this manifest's first version). Per requirement 1 ("removing support for a baseline is never incidental cleanup"), that rule protects a baseline that was actually DECLARED; this one never was, so there is nothing to migrate or remove support for.
